dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7793 IH: The Dalilah Law
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Houchin (for herself, Mr. Fong, and Mr. Obernolte) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure

form.official-title: To prohibit the issuance of commercial driver’s licenses to individuals who are not citizens or lawful permanent residents of the United States or holders of certain work visas, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as The Dalilah Law.

section.header: Prohibition on issuance of CDLs to individuals who are not citizens or lawful permanent residents of the United States or holders of certain work visas

paragraph.text: The term commercial driver’s license has the meaning given the term in section 31301 of title 49, United States Code.
The term commercial motor vehicle has the meaning given the term in section 31301 of title 49, United States Code.
The term covered examination means any test or examination relating to the issuance or renewal of a covered license or authorization, including—

subparagraph.text: a commercial driver’s license knowledge test;
a commercial driver’s license skills test; and
any other test or examination required to acquire, maintain, or upgrade a covered license or authorization.

paragraph.text: The term covered funding, with respect to a State, means any funding that is authorized to be provided by the Secretary to that State, or for a project or activity carried out in that State, under any provision of Federal law (including regulations).
The term covered license or authorization means—

subparagraph.text: a commercial driver’s license, including a non-domiciled commercial driver’s license; and
any other license or authorization issued by a State authorizing an individual to operate a commercial motor vehicle.

paragraph.text: The term non-domiciled commercial driver’s license means a commercial driver’s license issued by a State or other jurisdiction to an individual who is not domiciled in that State or jurisdiction, in accordance with part 383 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations).

subsection.text: Section 31311(a)(12) of title 49, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking who operates and inserting the following: “who—
in clause (i) (as so designated), by striking vehicle and is and inserting the following: “vehicle;
in clause (ii) (as so designated), by striking State. and inserting the following: “State; and

clause.text: is a citizen or lawful permanent resident of the United States.

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), by striking and at the end;
in clause (ii), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

subclause.text: is a citizen or lawful permanent resident of the United States; or

is a nonimmigrant described in subparagraph (E)(ii), (H)(ii)(a), or (H)(ii)(b) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101(a)(15)) and is in possession of a valid, unexpired nonimmigrant visa issued to the individual pursuant to any such subparagraph.

subparagraph.text: in clause (ii)(II), by striking and at the end;
in clause (iii), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

subclause.text: is a citizen or lawful permanent resident of the United States; or

is a nonimmigrant described in subparagraph (E)(ii), (H)(ii)(a), or (H)(ii)(b) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101(a)(15)) and is in possession of a valid, unexpired nonimmigrant visa issued to the individual pursuant to any such subparagraph.

subsection.text: Section 31310 of title 49, United States Code, is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking paragraph (9) of; and
by striking 7102(9) and inserting 7102; and

paragraph.text: by adding at the end the following:

subsection.header: Disqualification based on lack of citizenship, lawful permanent residence, or work visa status
subsection.text: The Secretary shall disqualify from operating a commercial motor vehicle for life an individual who operates a commercial motor vehicle in the United States while that individual is not a citizen or lawful permanent resident of the United States or a nonimmigrant described in subparagraph (E)(ii), (H)(ii)(a), or (H)(ii)(b) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101(a)(15)) in possession of a valid, unexpired nonimmigrant visa issued to the individual pursuant to any such subparagraph, unless such individual—

paragraph.text: is operating a commercial motor vehicle as a nonimmigrant described in subparagraph (B) of that section and is in possession of a valid, unexpired nonimmigrant visa issued to the individual pursuant to that subparagraph; or

holds a valid travel authorization pursuant to section 217.5 of title 8, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulation), and possesses a valid, unexpired admission record pursuant to section 1302 of title 8, United States Code.

subsection.text: To avoid the withholding of covered funding under paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (e), a State shall—

paragraph.text: require all individuals who, as of the date of enactment of this Act, hold a covered license or authorization issued by the State to be recertified for that covered license or authorization not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of this Act, which recertification shall include verification that the individual—

subparagraph.text: is—

clause.text: a citizen or lawful permanent resident of the United States; or

a nonimmigrant described in subparagraph (E)(ii), (H)(ii)(a), or (H)(ii)(b) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101(a)(15)) in possession of a valid, unexpired nonimmigrant visa issued to the individual pursuant to any such subparagraph;

subparagraph.text: is proficient in the English language, as described in section 391.11(b)(2) of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of enactment of this Act); and
has passed all covered examinations relating to the covered license or authorization in English; and

paragraph.text: revoke the covered license or authorization of any individual who—

subparagraph.text: fails to recertify by the deadline described in paragraph (1); or
on recertification under that paragraph, is found—

subclause.text: a citizen or lawful permanent resident of the United States; or

a nonimmigrant described in subparagraph (E)(ii), (H)(ii)(a), or (H)(ii)(b) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101(a)(15)) in possession of a valid, unexpired nonimmigrant visa issued to the individual pursuant to any such subparagraph;

clause.text: not to be proficient in the English language, as described in section 391.11(b)(2) of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on the date of enactment of this Act); or
not to have passed all covered examinations relating to the covered license or authorization in English.

paragraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, beginning with the first fiscal year beginning after the deadline for recertifications under subsection (d)(1), the Secretary shall withhold all covered funding from a State that fails to complete those recertifications by that deadline.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, beginning with the first fiscal year beginning after the deadline for recertifications under subsection (d)(1), the Secretary shall withhold all covered funding from a State that fails to complete the revocations described in subsection (d)(2) by that deadline.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, beginning with the first fiscal year beginning after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall withhold all covered funding from a State that, after that date of enactment, issues covered licenses or authorizations to individuals who are neither—

subparagraph.text: citizens or lawful permanent residents of the United States; or

nonimmigrants described in subparagraph (E)(ii), (H)(ii)(a), or (H)(ii)(b) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1101(a)(15)) in possession of a valid, unexpired nonimmigrant visa issued to such individuals pursuant to any such subparagraph.

paragraph.header: Witholding for English proficiency verification failure
paragraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, beginning with the first fiscal year beginning after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall withhold all covered funding from a State that, after that date of enactment—

subparagraph.text: issues covered licenses or authorizations to individuals who are not proficient in the English language, as described in section 391.11(b)(2) of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (or a successor regulation); or
administers any covered examination in any language other than English.
